reasoning_content:
The user wants the layout of the provided document page extracted into a JSON object with bounding boxes.

1.  **Identify the main sections:**
    *   Header: Part number, document ID, date, company logo.
    *   Title: "5.5 Electrical Characteristics (continued)"
    *   Description: Operating conditions text.
    *   Table: The main content, containing parameters, test conditions, min/typ/max values, and units.
    *   Footer: Page number, feedback link, copyright, product folder link.
    *   Footnote: "(1) VIN = 1.45V for VOUT < 0.9V"

2.  **Process the Header:**
    *   "TLV757P": Top left.
    *   "SBVS322C – OCTOBER 2017 – REVISED MARCH 2024": Below part number.
    *   "TEXAS INSTRUMENTS" logo and URL: Top right.

3.  **Process the Title and Description:**
    *   "5.5 Electrical Characteristics (continued)": Bold header.
    *   "over operating free-air temperature range...": Paragraph text.

4.  **Process the Table:**
    *   This is the most complex part. I need to break it down row by row or logical block by block.
    *   **Header Row:** PARAMETER, TEST CONDITIONS, MIN, TYP, MAX, UNIT.
    *   **Row Group 1 (VDO - Dropout voltage):**
        *   This spans many rows. The "VDO" and "Dropout voltage" cells span the entire height of this section.
        *   The "TEST CONDITIONS" column is split into two main blocks based on temperature:
            *   Block 1: "IOUT = 1A, -40°C ≤ TJ ≤ +85°C"
            *   Block 2: "IOUT = 1A, -40°C ≤ TJ ≤ +125°C"
        *   Inside these blocks, there are specific voltage ranges (e.g., "0.6V ≤ VOUT < 0.8V").
        *   The values (MIN, TYP, MAX) correspond to these ranges.
        *   The "UNIT" column has "mV" spanning the whole VDO section.
    *   **Row Group 2 (PSRR):**
        *   Parameter: PSRR
        *   Description: Power supply rejection ratio
        *   Conditions: f = 1kHz..., f = 100kHz..., f = 1MHz...
        *   Values: 52, 46, 52
        *   Unit: dB
    *   **Row Group 3 (Vn):**
        *   Parameter: Vn
        *   Description: Output noise voltage
        *   Conditions: BW = 10Hz to 100kHz...
        *   Value: 71.5
        *   Unit: µVRMS
    *   **Row Group 4 (VUVLO):**
        *   Parameter: VUVLO
        *   Description: Undervoltage lockout
        *   Condition: VIN rising
        *   Values: 1.21, 1.3, 1.44
        *   Unit: V
    *   **Row Group 5 (VUVLO, HYST):**
        *   Parameter: VUVLO, HYST
        *   Description: Undervoltage lockout hysteresis
        *   Condition: VIN falling
        *   Value: 40
        *   Unit: mV
    *   **Row Group 6 (tSTR):**
        *   Parameter: tSTR
        *   Description: Startup time
        *   Value: 550
        *   Unit: µs
    *   **Row Group 7 (VHI):**
        *   Parameter: VHI
        *   Description: EN pin high voltage (enabled)
        *   Value: 1
        *   Unit: V
    *   **Row Group 8 (VLO):**
        *   Parameter: VLO
        *   Description: EN pin low voltage (enabled)
        *   Value: 0.3
        *   Unit: V
    *   **Row Group 9 (IEN):**
        *   Parameter: IEN
        *   Description: Enable pin current
        *   Condition: VIN = 5.5V, EN = 5.5V
        *   Value: 10
        *   Unit: nA
    *   **Row Group 10 (RPULLDOWN):**
        *   Parameter: RPULLDOWN
        *   Description: Pulldown resistance
        *   Condition: VIN = 3.3V (P version only)
        *   Value: 95
        *   Unit: Ω
    *   **Row Group 11 (TSD):**
        *   Parameter: TSD
        *   Description: Thermal shutdown
        *   Conditions: Shutdown, temperature increasing / Reset, temperature decreasing
        *   Values: 165 / 155
        *   Unit: °C

5.  **Process Footer and Footnote:**
    *   Footnote: "(1) VIN = 1.45V for VOUT < 0.9V"
    *   Footer line: "6 Submit Document Feedback Copyright © 2024 Texas Instruments Incorporated Product Folder Links: TLV757P"

6.  **Construct JSON:** Assemble all identified elements with their coordinates. I will group the table rows logically.
